name: Figma AI Agent
summary: purpose-built AI agent embedded in Figma's canvas — natural-language prompts generate and remix designs, automate rote work, and respect your design system; fire up multiple agents to do different tasks in parallel, plus new partnerships baking Claude Code and Codex into the design surface; rolling out in beta for Full seats on Pro/Org/Enterprise

name: Stitch 2.0
summary: Google Labs' AI-native UI design tool gets 'vibe design' — a real-time streaming agent that renders components onto an infinite canvas as you type or speak, plus multiplayer editing; voice input lets you say 'show me three menu options' and watch the canvas respond; free, no credit card, runs on Gemini 2.5 Flash/Pro

name: Antigravity 2.0
summary: Google's new agent-first dev platform unveiled at I/O — standalone desktop app for orchestrating parallel agents, Go-based CLI, SDK, and Managed Agents in the Gemini API that spin up isolated Linux sandboxes in one call; scheduled tasks turn agents into persistent background pipelines, native Android app generation from a prompt, and Google Play Console publishing baked into AI Studio; $100/mo AI Ultra plan adds 5x usage limits

name: Gemini Omni Flash
summary: Google's first unified multimodal generation model — accepts image, audio, video, and text in a single prompt and reasons across them to produce video you can keep editing through conversation; rolling out worldwide to Gemini app subscribers (AI Plus/Pro/Ultra) and the Flow creative tool, plus free access for YouTube Shorts and YouTube Create users

name: Cursor Composer 2.5
summary: Cursor's in-house coding model — matches Claude Opus 4.7 and GPT-5.5 on SWE-Bench Multilingual at $0.50/M input tokens, roughly a tenth of Anthropic's pricing; built for long tool-heavy sessions across files, terminal, and tests; included usage doubled the first week post-launch (~May 25)

name: Codex in ChatGPT Mobile
summary: Codex lands in the ChatGPT mobile app on iOS and Android — kick off coding tasks, review diffs, redirect runs, and approve agent actions from your phone while Codex executes on a connected Mac; preview across all plans including free, with 4M+ weekly Codex users

name: Raindrop Workshop
summary: open-source local debugger and eval tool for AI agents — streams every token, tool call, and decision to a localhost dashboard the moment it happens, then writes traces to a single SQL file your coding agent can query to write its own evals; MIT-licensed, one-line install, works with Vercel AI SDK, OpenAI Agents, Anthropic SDK, LangChain, LangGraph, CrewAI, Pydantic AI

name: Claude for Small Business
summary: packaged Claude for solopreneurs and small teams — prebuilt agentic workflows and one-click connectors into QuickBooks, PayPal, HubSpot, Canva, DocuSign, Google Workspace, and Microsoft 365 to handle payroll, month-end close, invoice chasing, and campaign creation; bundled with a free AI fluency course
